page: Microsoft Azure E2s_v4 is an entry-level virtual machine within the ESv4 family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, configured with 2 virtual CPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachment.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the average range, supported by single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, due to the limited physical core count, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU score rank in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests also register in the lower percentiles, with uncached read and write speeds falling in the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving benchmarks reflect the constraints of the dual-vCPU configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2s_v4 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity, without the overhead of higher vCPU counts. It is best suited for lightweight, memory-intensive applications, small-scale databases, development environments, or caching layers where memory capacity is prioritized over high-throughput parallel processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations rank in the bottom 10% due to the single physical core configuration. The instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing high-performance external disk attachment. It represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-sensitive workloads with low parallel processing demands, such as small databases, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v4 is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL CPU. It is Premium Storage capable and suited for low-compute, memory-sensitive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, with no local storage.
tagline: A low-vCPU, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-sensitive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
High memory allocation of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for small databases, caching, and memory-sensitive testing
